steps.title: Translate the decay percentage to a decay factor
steps.content: If the mass decreases by 22% every 11.11 minutes, then 22% is lost and 78% remains. The decay factor per 11.11-minute period is $0.78$, not $0.22$. This immediately eliminates choices A and B.

steps.title: Determine the exponent form
steps.content: In an exponential decay model, the exponent represents the number of time periods that have elapsed. Since one period = 11.11 minutes and t is measured in minutes, the number of periods after t minutes is $\frac{t}{11.11}$. The exponent cannot be $t + 11.11$, which eliminates choice C.

steps.title: Write the complete model
steps.content:
The standard form is mass = (initial mass) × (decay factor)number of periods.

With initial mass $100$ grams, decay factor $0.78$, and exponent $\frac{t}{11.11}$: $$M = 100(0.78)^{\frac{t}{11.11}}$$ Verify at $t = 11.11$: $M = 100(0.78)^{1} = 78$ grams, confirming the 22% loss.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
